name: Richie Lending
description_short: Richie AI is an embedded lending infrastructure platform that enables SaaS platforms and marketplaces to offer small business loans to their merchants in under an hour.
description_long:
Richie AI, founded in 2023, operates as an embedded lending infrastructure provider designed specifically for B2B SaaS platforms, marketplaces, and payment processors. The company recognizes that small and midsize business owners—particularly those from marginalized communities—face barriers accessing traditional bank financing and instead seek lending solutions from vendors they already trust within their existing platforms.

Richie AI does not directly lend money to consumers; instead, it provides white-label lending technology that SaaS platforms integrate to become lenders themselves. Their service includes loan application forms, application management systems, private-labeled merchant portals, access to a network of 100+ platform partners and 109+ lenders, and 9+ loan products. Merchants can access 24-hour funding and compare offers from multiple lenders. The platform emphasizes data sharing, payment splitting options, and interest rate reductions of up to 50% for merchants.

What distinguishes Richie is their focus on serving underrepresented entrepreneurs—business owners of color, immigrants, and female entrepreneurs—while maintaining accessibility through multiple integration options: no-code (subdomain attachment in one hour), some-code (widget insertion), and full API solutions. They offer anonymous prequalification with soft credit pulls, encrypted personal information, and dedicated 24/7 customer support. The company allows platform partners to retain merchant relationships, generate ancillary revenue, and customize the entire experience through their fully private-labeled solution.

However, Richie AI is a B2B technology provider, not a direct lender. Consumers cannot directly apply for loans through Richie; they must access loans through their integrated partner platforms. The pricing page is incomplete on the public site, making plan details unclear. Their business model depends entirely on SaaS platform adoption, and actual loan terms, rates, and approval odds depend on the lenders in their network rather than Richie itself. Success requires merchant platforms to actively promote the lending program to their users.
address: 140 West St 2nd floor, New York, NY 10007

company_info.headquarters: 140 West St 2nd floor, New York, NY 10007

pros: Enables 24-hour business loan funding through integrated platforms
Merchants can compare offers from 109+ lenders and access 9+ loan products simultaneously
Interest savings up to 50% through data sharing and lender network competition
No-code integration available—platforms can launch in under one hour without technical resources
Anonymous prequalification with soft credit pulls that don't impact merchant credit scores
Full data encryption and privacy protections with masked phone numbers during prequalification
Serves underrepresented entrepreneurs (people of color, immigrants, female business owners) explicitly
Private-labeled solution keeps merchants on partner platform throughout lending process
cons: Richie AI does not directly lend—it only provides technology infrastructure; actual loan terms depend on connected lenders
Merchants must access loans through their SaaS platform partner, not directly from Richie; limited consumer-facing brand recognition
Pricing is opaque on their public website; full plan details and costs not disclosed for prospective partners
Success depends on SaaS platform adoption and merchant promotion; inactive platforms generate no loan volume
Soft-pull pre-approvals still require hard credit pulls for final underwriting, which will impact business credit
best_for: SaaS platforms and online marketplaces seeking new revenue streams by offering financing to their merchant customers
Small business owners accessing loans through established platforms they already use and trust
Underrepresented entrepreneurs (business owners of color, immigrants, female entrepreneurs) facing barriers at traditional banks
Payment processors wanting to deepen merchant relationships and provide embedded financial services
diagnosis: Richie AI is best for SaaS platforms and marketplaces that want to add embedded lending as a revenue-generating feature and deeper merchant retention tool, not for individual consumers seeking direct loans. The critical caveat is that consumers cannot apply directly to Richie AI; they access loans exclusively through their platform partner, making Richie a B2B infrastructure play rather than a consumer lender.
services: White-labeled loan application forms and merchant portals
Application management system (AMS) for loan processing and tracking
Access to network of 100+ platform partners and 109+ institutional lenders
9+ business loan products (specific products not detailed on website)
Multi-lender comparison tool for merchants to see competing offers
Data sharing infrastructure to reduce lender risk and lower interest rates
Payment splitting options (platform subsidizes portion of merchant payments)
No-code integration (subdomain-based launch in under 1 hour)
Widget-based integration (code snippet insertion for website placement)
Full API access for custom merchant experience build-outs
24/7 customer success support and dedicated deal processing support
